caption:
9/11: Does The Official Story Add Up? 🏙️

They really expect us to believe 19 cave dwellers with box cutters and a satellite phone outsmarted the entire US military on the most defended airspace in the world… while living wild lifestyles with strippers and coke? 🚨

One guy who could barely fly a Cessna allegedly pulled a perfect 270° corkscrew maneuver into the exact Pentagon office investigating $2.3 TRILLION in missing funds — the day after it was announced. ⚡

A passport magically survives a fiery plane crash to land blocks away… yet the black boxes don’t? Buildings collapse at freefall speed into their own footprint. And WTC7? The official story barely mentions it. 🤔

Dare to notice the holes. The official narrative isn’t just flawed — it feels like an insult to basic intelligence. Who really benefited? The questions remain unanswered 20+ years later.

Drop 🔥 if you see through the script. Comment “INSIDE JOB” if you’re not buying it anymore. Save this. Share this before it gets buried. Truth still matters. 🤫
audio_track_actual: Single male narrator delivering a rapid-fire monologue against archival news footage from September 11, 2001 and the Iraq war that followed.
video_overlay_text_observed: Archival cable-news clips of WTC 1 burning, the Pentagon strike, and post-collapse coverage; on-screen graphics naming Rumsfeld's $2.3 trillion statement, Able Danger, WTC 7, Ptech, Sibel Edmonds, and the 9/11 Commission's 'failure of imagination' finding.
audio_content_summary: The narrator catalogs items the reel argues were minimized, omitted, or contradicted by the official 9/11 account: Defense Secretary Donald Rumsfeld's September 10, 2001 announcement that the Pentagon could not track $2.3 trillion in transactions; the alleged piloting limitations of the hijackers (notably Hani Hanjour and the Pentagon-strike maneuver); the survival of Satam al-Suqami's passport in lower Manhattan; the late-afternoon collapse of WTC 7; the Able Danger military data-mining program said to have identified Mohamed Atta before the attacks; the Ptech software company tied to disputed terror-financing investigations; FBI translator-whistleblower Sibel Edmonds and the state-secrets order imposed on her; and the 9/11 Commission's 'failure of imagination' conclusion. Each of these named threads has its own documented public record. The reel's editorial proposition is that, taken together, they amount to a cover-up; the documented record is what is captured in the linked evidence below.
implied_frame: The reel argues that the official 9/11 account is internally inconsistent at multiple named points. Several of those named points — Rumsfeld's $2.3 trillion announcement, Sibel Edmonds, WTC 7, the Suqami passport, Hanjour's piloting — are matters of documented public record (linked below) and can be examined individually. Whether the sum of those facts amounts to a cover-up is the inference the reel asks the reader to draw; the documented facts themselves are the reader's starting point.
capture_method: Headless browser recording of the in-page <video> element via MediaRecorder over captureStream(), viewport 1280x800.
